skill.name: meituan-printer
skill.description: 美团热敏打印机技能。向美团热敏打印机发送格式化任务单、检查单或小票。 支持通过预配置别名（中文）或直接指定 IP:端口来选择目标打印机。 触发场景：用户要求打印、发送到打印机、打印任务单/检查单/小票、 提到"打印机"、"后厨打印"、"前厅打单"、"素菜岗打印"、 "配置打印机"、"管理打印机"、"添加打印机"、"新增打印机"、"删除打印机"、 "定时打印"、"自动打印"等。
skill.summary: Send formatted task lists, inspection forms, or receipts to Meituan thermal printers via TCP/IP.
skill.author: chenyangshu

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate printer integration skill for Meituan thermal receipt printers. Static findings are largely false positives or over-classifications. Hex-encoded bytes are standard ESC/POS printer commands, not obfuscation. External commands are for legitimate printer operations. The risk level is MEDIUM due to network access to local printers, shell command execution for printer management, and web interface functionality.

high_findings.title: Hex-encoded ESC/POS Commands
high_findings.description: The skill uses hex-encoded byte sequences (like b'\x1B\x40') which are standard ESC/POS printer control commands. These are NOT obfuscation - they are the industry-standard protocol for thermal receipt printers. This is false positive from static scanner.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: ESC/POS hex commands (0x1B 0x40 for init, 0x1B 0x61 for alignment, 0x1B 0x21 for font size) are industry-standard thermal printer protocol. Scanner misidentifies legitimate printer commands as obfuscation.

high_findings.title: System Task Scheduling (launchd/schtasks)
high_findings.description: The skill can create macOS launchd agents and Windows scheduled tasks for automated printing. This is an intentional feature, not a blocker. User consent is required before system-level task creation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess.run calls with launchctl and schtasks are for legitimate scheduled printing functionality. This is a documented feature in SKILL.md requiring user authorization.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (MD5)
high_findings.description: Code references MD5 for printer authentication. However, this is the legacy protocol used by Meituan thermal printers - not a security vulnerability of the skill itself but a limitation of the hardware protocol.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 is used for legacy Meituan printer protocol compatibility. This reflects the hardware manufacturer's protocol limitation, not an introduced vulnerability.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded IP Addresses in Examples
medium_findings.description: Example configurations contain hardcoded IP addresses (192.168.x.x). These are example placeholders in config.example.json and documentation, not actual credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: IPs in config.example.json are clearly labeled example placeholders (192.168.3.x range is typical for private LAN). No actual credentials exposed.

medium_findings.title: innerHTML Usage in Web Interface
medium_findings.description: The web admin interface uses innerHTML for dynamic content rendering. While this is a potential XSS vector, the interface runs on localhost only and does not process external user input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Local-only web interface limits exposure. XSS would require local system access already compromised. However, proper input sanitization should be verified.

medium_findings.title: Python subprocess.run for Printer Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill uses subprocess.run to execute printer-related commands. Arguments are constructed from config files, not direct user input, reducing injection risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess calls construct commands from structured config data. Task parameters come from internal JSON files, not untrusted external input. However, should use list-based arguments when possible.

medium_findings.title: Temporary Directory Access
medium_findings.description: The skill writes temporary log files to /tmp directory for scheduled task debugging. Standard temp file usage with user-owned paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Temp files in /tmp/meituan-printer-{task_id} are for task logging purposes. Standard temporary file pattern with user-specific paths.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation contains hardcoded URLs for local service endpoints (localhost:5000). These are standard for local development/demo purposes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: localhost URLs in documentation are standard for local service endpoints. No external network access to untrusted servers.

low_findings.title: Environment Configuration Access
low_findings.description: The skill reads environment configuration for Python path detection and platform identification. Standard environment variable usage for cross-platform compatibility.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment access is for platform detection and Python command lookup. No sensitive environment variables are accessed or exposed.

content.user_title: Print task lists and receipts to Meituan thermal printers
content.value_statement: Restaurant staff struggle to manually print task lists and receipts for kitchen operations. This skill enables AI assistants to send formatted task lists, inspection forms, and receipts directly to Meituan thermal printers over the local network.
actual_capabilities: Send formatted task lists and inspection forms to Meituan thermal printers via TCP/IP network
Manage multiple printers using Chinese aliases (e.g., 'kitchen', 'front-desk') for easy selection
Test printer connectivity and verify printer is reachable before printing
Create and manage scheduled print tasks with configurable frequency (daily, weekly, monthly)
Export scheduled tasks to system-level schedulers (launchd on macOS, Task Scheduler on Windows)
Provide web-based admin interface for browser-based printer management
limitations: Printers must be on the same local network as the computer running the skill
Does not support cloud-based or remote printer access
Print content is text-based; complex graphics or images are not supported
System-level task export requires administrator privileges

use_cases.title: Kitchen task list printing
use_cases.description: Restaurant kitchen staff can ask the AI to print task lists for specific stations (e.g., 'print vegetable station task list'). The AI formats and sends the task list to the correct printer.

use_cases.title: Scheduled shift reminder printing
use_cases.description: Configure automatic daily or weekly printing of inspection checklists. The AI helps set up scheduled tasks that run at specific times without manual intervention.

use_cases.title: Receipt printing for customer orders
use_cases.description: Print formatted receipts for customer orders at the front desk printer. The AI helps generate and send properly formatted receipts.

prompt_templates.title: Simple print request
prompt_templates.prompt: Print the following task list on the kitchen printer: [task content]
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic one-time printing to a configured printer

prompt_templates.title: Print with specific printer
prompt_templates.prompt: Send this inspection checklist to the vegetable station printer: [content]
prompt_templates.scenario: Selecting a specific printer by its configured alias

prompt_templates.title: Add new printer
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a new printer with IP [IP address], port [port], and call it [alias]
prompt_templates.scenario: Configuring a new printer for the first time

prompt_templates.title: Schedule recurring print task
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a daily print task that prints [content] at [time] on the [printer name] printer
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating automated scheduled printing for regular tasks

output_examples.input:
Print this shift checklist on the kitchen printer: Morning shift tasks:
-------09:00-------
Open station
Check inventory
-------10:00-------
Restock items
output_examples.output: Successfully sent task list to kitchen printer (192.168.3.100:9100). The formatted task list will print with timestamp, station header, and time-blocked sections.

output_examples.input: Add a new printer with IP 192.168.3.150, port 9100, and call it 'dessert station'
output_examples.output: Printer 'dessert station' added successfully. Connection test: reachable. You can now print to this printer using the alias 'dessert station'.

Configured printers:
| Alias | IP Address | Port | Type |
|-------|-----------|------|------|
| kitchen | 192.168.3.100 | 9100 | main kitchen |
| dessert station | 192.168.3.150 | 9100 | dessert |
| front desk | 192.168.3.200 | 9100 | reception |

best_practices: Use descriptive Chinese aliases for printers to make selection intuitive (e.g., '后厨', '素菜岗', '前厅酒水')
Test printer connectivity before attempting first print to ensure proper configuration
Keep printers on a stable local network segment for reliable operation
anti_patterns: Do not print confidential information such as customer payment details or personal data
Avoid printing extremely large documents; the skill is optimized for task lists and receipts
Do not share printer IP addresses with unauthorized users as they could send unwanted print jobs

faq.question: How do I configure a new printer?
faq.answer: Tell the AI the printer's IP address, port (usually 9100), and choose an alias. The AI will test the connection and save the configuration.

faq.question: Can I print images or logos?
faq.answer: No, this skill supports text-based printing only. Complex graphics cannot be printed on standard thermal receipt printers.

faq.question: How do scheduled tasks work?
faq.answer: You can set up daily, weekly, or monthly print tasks. The skill can export these to your operating system's task scheduler for reliable execution.

faq.question: What if the printer is not on the same network?
faq.answer: The printer must be accessible on your local network. VPN or network bridging may be needed for remote printer access (not officially supported).

faq.question: How do I use the web admin interface?
faq.answer: Run the start.command script on macOS or start.bat on Windows, then open http://localhost:5000 in your browser.

faq.question: What printer brands are supported?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for Meituan thermal receipt printers using the ESC/POS protocol. Other printers supporting ESC/POS may also work.
